focus: maintainability closure for stale paths, generated metadata integrity, command authority consistency, and file-format follow-up

findingDetails.title: The database module README sends every operator command to the removed _db tree
findingDetails.evidence: All five supported operator examples invoke absent styles/_db/operator.mjs while the executable lives at styles/lib/database/operator.mjs.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Manifest-scoped sweep plus direct path checks proved five stale command examples, no compatibility executable, and the relocated operator present.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace live operational references with centralized current paths and execute each documented command form.

claimAdjudication.claim: The current database module README presents five supported maintenance commands that all target a removed executable path.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked for a compatibility executable and confirmed only the relocated current operator exists.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: The paths could be historical, but they occur under live Operator Commands and Adapter Modes sections.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A proven compatibility resolver makes every literal command executable or this README is explicitly retired.

findingDetails.title: Generated packet graphs still encode pre-rewrite status and pre-restructure paths
findingDetails.evidence: The 012 graph remains in_progress despite IMPLEMENTED authority, while 001/004 graphs expose removed _db files as key files and entities.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Manifest-listed metadata parsed successfully; direct status/path comparison found stale current-state graph fields across 012, 001, and 004.
findingDetails.recommendation: Regenerate metadata from corrected canonical docs and verify every emitted target and lifecycle state.

claimAdjudication.claim: Generated graph metadata supplies stale lifecycle state and removed file targets after the migrations.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Compared generated state with implementation authority and checked graph targets against the relocated tree.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Historical prose may preserve old paths, but generated graph routing fields are current-state surfaces.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: All consumers are proven to ignore these fields and another current authority supplies correct state and targets.

traceabilityChecks.evidence: Current database README retains executable _db commands.
Manifest-listed graph metadata contains stale lifecycle state and dead _db entities.
Direct command/presentation reads agree and command-contract tests passed 12/12.
git diff --check emitted no warning at pinned HEAD.

noveltyJustification: Two new P1 surfaces add 10 weighted points to 25 accumulated active weighted points; they are distinct from the existing manual-playbook and parent-map findings.
edgeCases: Historical migration and fixture paths were ruled out as intentional.
001 openly records pending metadata refresh; this does not make current graph state valid.
One exact search exceeded the manifest boundary; out-of-manifest hits were discarded and no finding relies on them.
integrationEvidence: operator README/executable, graph metadata, and command/presentation pair
structuralImpact: unavailable: Code Graph disabled by dispatch contract; direct manifest-scoped fallback used

fallback: direct reads, manifest-scoped exact search, pinned Git evidence, and focused executable tests

reason: The governing restructure spec explicitly retains them as path-override fixtures.
They identify before/after or reference-only migration evidence rather than live commands.
Direct command/presentation reads agree and the command-contract test passed 12/12.

detail: One exact-search command covered the wider sk-design graph-metadata tree; all out-of-manifest hits were discarded and no finding relies on them.
